I'm not mad that this is one of the very few instances where smooth, cartoonish, traditional animation is used nowadays in a film, even if it's such a shame that this particular medium has been steadily declining ever since the 90s. I'm not mad it has themes in poor taste, there's plenty of movies that manage to pull that off in their plots. I'm not mad it has needlessly explicit visuals, I have also watched plenty of movies that embrace the crassness of genitals and sexual acts in a way that enhances the film as a whole. I'm not mad about such a simple premise, because a short argument can have a lot to tell.

No, I'm simply disappointed about the fact that this film exists. Because it's not one single thing that makes me thoroughly uninterested in this piece of media, but the conjunction of all factors combined, and the added stinger that the talent of the animators is going to be wasted in 120 minutes of nothing.

This would be a nice animated short. Let the animators loose, have them be as silky smooth as they have been shown capable of in this trailer. Let the premise untouched, the characters as-is, the crassness and gratuituous visuals in full display. But don't make us sit through that for longer than 10 minutes.

It feels like the 2003 Cat in the Hat wrote these jokes if I’m being completely honest but even then he got in an occasional funny one. Bound to happen with this movie too (infinite monkeys, infinite typewriters, you know the drill) but I do not see it becoming a masterpiece for some in the same way the Cat in the Hat (2003) managed to be for some.
